meta.title: Wind Turbine Work at Height + Rescue Plan
meta.subtitle: WTG-12, Wind Farm WF-7 — nacelle gearbox inspection
meta.author: HSE Renewables
meta.deid_notice: All personnel are referred to by role label and GWO competence is cited as a requirement (certificate numbers are not reproduced). Identifiers were pseudonymised before drafting; the re-identification key is held separately and is not part of this document.

sections.text: This plan governs nacelle gearbox-inspection work at height on WTG-12 at Wind Farm WF-7. The component cannot be brought to ground, so work at height is unavoidable; control therefore leads with collective fall-prevention (the nacelle working platform and roof guardrail) before personal fall-arrest, per WAH Regs 2005 reg 6/7. A tested, team-owned rescue recovers a worker suspended in a harness within 10 minutes by a two-person-minimum climb team with ground support. Calling 999 is a parallel supplement, not the rescue plan.

rows.control: AVOID work at height — gearbox borescope inspection tested for ground-level/remote option; component cannot be lowered, so at-height work is unavoidable (avoidance test recorded)
PREVENT a fall — nacelle internal working platform + roof-hatch guardrail (collective) used before any personal fall-arrest
MITIGATE / ARREST — harness + energy-absorbing lanyard on the rated nacelle anchor for the roof-hatch transition only

rows: Team-owned descent/haul kit (rescue device + ground anchor) per GWO ART method, drilled quarterly
Two-person-minimum climb team + one ground-support person able to initiate the rescue
999 called in parallel by Ground Support — SUPPLEMENT only, NOT the rescue plan
sections.caption: Rescue plan — tested, team-owned, recovery within 10 minutes; 999 is a supplement only

sections.text: A WAH plan with no tested rescue is rejected (reg 4). 'Call the emergency services and wait' is NOT a rescue plan — suspension trauma can be fatal within minutes.

rows.title: Original method statement named '999 / site first-aider' as the rescue arrangement
rows.description: The prior method statement relied on the emergency services and a ground-based first-aider with no team-owned at-height recovery capability — rejected; replaced with a tested team-owned 10-minute recovery.

rows.title: Roof-hatch transition had no collective protection
rows.description: The roof-hatch transition relied on personal fall-arrest only; a temporary guardrail is now specified as collective prevention before the harness limb.

sections.text: Weather hold / stop thresholds (owned by REN-03)

items: Hub-height wind cut-off ~= 15 m/s [ASSUMED — confirm with weather-dynamic-risk-assessment (REN-03); proposed-and-user-confirmed, not a citation]
Lightning stand-down per NFPA 780 practice / the site lightning-warning service [GAP — detection radius + time site-confirmed]
No personnel-carrier lift used for this task, so the BS 7121-1 (2016) 7 m/s man-riding ceiling does not apply

sections.text: Both climbers and the Rescue Lead hold current GWO Working at Heights, First Aid and Advanced Rescue Training competence, refreshed every 2 years. Certificate numbers are a licensed competence-scheme identifier and are NOT reproduced here ([GAP]); competence is verified against a separate access-controlled training record before the climb.

rows.action: Drill the team-owned suspended-worker rescue on WTG-12 access and record the measured recovery time
Add the temporary roof-hatch guardrail to the equipment list and the pre-climb checklist
Confirm the hub-height wind hold value with REN-03 and the turbine OEM in-service limit

items: GWO Working at Heights / First Aid / Advanced Rescue Training (competence requirement)
